flashcards.back: |a| is the distance from a to zero on the number line. Always non-negative.
|−7| = 7. Absolute value of a negative number is its opposite (positive).
|a| = a if a ≥ 0, and |a| = −a if a < 0.

flashcards.front: What property of absolute value relates to opposite numbers?
flashcards.back: |a| = |−a|. Opposite numbers have the same absolute value because they're equidistant from zero.
